statement: NVIDIA is offering fast-growing startups compute access in exchange for a share of future revenue, with two initial partners (Sharon AI, Firmus) providing access to more than 200,000 GPUs; NVIDIA raised $25 billion in bonds on June 15, 2026 to fund the program at scale.
why: Vendor-financed compute-for-revenue-share pulls forward GPU demand from liquidity-constrained AI firms while deepening NVIDIA's structural dependence on funded customers.

impact.mechanism: Revenue-share financing underwrites neocloud GPU deployments, sustaining demand but concentrating counterparty and circular-financing risk.

evidence.excerpt: Chipmaker Nvidia says it will offer fast-growing start-ups the chance to swap access to compute power for a slice of future profits.

confidence.basis: Single secondary publisher (CNBC); underlying NVIDIA blog not independently corroborated across three separate publishers.

dispersion: conflicting same-key reports: statement/trend changed (trend rising -> rising); sources: CNBC, NVIDIA SEC Form 10-Q
